title: Cold Sores
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Cold sores are caused by herpes simplex virus and commonly occur around the mouth or face.
Outbreaks can be recurrent and may be triggered by stress, illness, or sun exposure.
Eye involvement, genital involvement, newborn exposure, or immunocompromised-host context requires clinician-directed handling.
